fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False, and skips clamping when clamp is True (the default). It should be `if clamp:` to clamp when clamping is enabled.

spec.expr: (clamp and True) or (not clamp and True)

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the branch body (clamping logic) should execute; the condition `not clamp` is inverted so clamping happens when clamp=False instead of True.
The then-branch should only execute when clamp is False; if clamp is True we should not be clamping here (but the inverted condition causes clamping when clamp=False).

spec.expr: not clamp and (y__2 >= min(y0, y1)) and (y__2 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: y__2 is the clamped value and should only exist (be computed) when clamp is False due to the inverted condition bug.
The else-branch (no clamping applied) should execute when clamp is True, but with the inverted condition the else-branch runs when clamp=True, skipping clamping entirely.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or ((__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1)) and (__cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1)))

constraints.intent: When clamping is enabled (clamp=True), the returned value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
